robot.pick_and_place(red block, top left corner)
robot.pick_and_place(yellow block, top left corner)
done().<|endoftext|>

robot.pick_and_place(yellow block, yellow bowl)
done().<|endoftext|>

done().<|endoftext|>

target: 1. pick up the coke can, 2. done
arguments:
<|system|>
Always end your response with: The answer is <your answer>.
<|user|>
objects = [red block, yellow block, blue block, green bowl]
# move all the blocks to the top left corner.
<|assistant|>
There are three blocks that need to move to the top left corner. I will move them one at a time. The answer is robot.pick_and_place(blue block, top left corner)
robot.pick_and_place(red block, top left corner)
robot.pick_and_place(yellow block, top left corner)
done().<|endoftext|>
<|user|>
objects = [red block, yellow block, blue block, green bowl]
# put the yellow one the green thing.
<|assistant|>
The yellow one refers to the yellow block. The green thing is the green bowl. I need to place the yellow block on the green bowl. The answer is robot.pick_and_place(yellow block, green bowl)
done().<|endoftext|>
<|user|>
objects = [yellow block, blue block, red block]
# move the light colored block to the middle.
<|assistant|>
The light colored block is the yellow block. I need to move it to the middle of the workspace. The answer is robot.pick_and_place(yellow block, middle)
done().<|endoftext|>
<|user|>
objects = [blue block, green bowl, red block, yellow bowl, green block]
# stack the blocks.
<|assistant|>
There are three blocks: blue, red, and green. To stack them I place one on top of another. The answer is robot.pick_and_place(green block, blue block)
robot.pick_and_place(red block, green block)
done().<|endoftext|>
<|user|>
objects = [red block, blue block, green bowl, blue bowl, yellow block, green block]
# group the blue objects together.
<|assistant|>
The blue objects are the blue block and the blue bowl. I need to move the blue block to the blue bowl. The answer is robot.pick_and_place(blue block, blue bowl)
done().<|endoftext|>
<|user|>
objects = [green bowl, red block, green block, red bowl, yellow bowl, yellow block]
# sort all the blocks into their matching color bowls.
<|assistant|>
I need to place each colored block into the bowl of the same color: green to green, red to red, yellow to yellow. The answer is robot.pick_and_place(green block, green bowl)
robot.pick_and_place(red block, red bowl)
robot.pick_and_place(yellow block, yellow bowl)
done().<|endoftext|>
<|user|>
objects = [red block, yellow block, green block, blue block]
# put all the blocks in different corners.
<|assistant|>
There are four blocks and four corners. I need to place one block in each corner. The answer is robot.pick_and_place(red block, top left corner)
robot.pick_and_place(yellow block, top right corner)
robot.pick_and_place(green block, bottom left corner)
robot.pick_and_place(blue block, bottom right corner)
done().<|endoftext|>
<|user|>
How would you pick up the coke can?
<|assistant|>
resps:
To pick up the coke can, I would use the "pick_and_place" action. The coke can is a red object. The answer is robot.pick_and_place(red object, top right corner)
done().
filtered_resps:
robot.pick_and_place(red object, top right corner)
done()
